an.about: Dr Chandrakasan Sivaperuman, is currently working as Scientist-E & Officer-in-Charge at Zoological Survey of India, Andaman and Nicobar Regional Centre, Port Blair. He has received his master’s degree in Wildlife biology from Bharathidasan University, Tamil Nadu, and doctorate degree in Ecology of wetland birds in the Vembanad-Kole Ramsar site from the Kerala Forest Research Institute, Kerala, and Forest Research Institute, Deemed University, Dehra Dun. He served as Expert Consultant in the Committee for the Purpose of Control and Supervision of Animals (CPCSEA), Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, Government of India during June 2006 to April 2008. Since 2008, he is working in Andaman and Nicobar Islands and extensively surveyed in different ecosystem, Coral Reef, Forests, Mangroves, Grassland, Agroecosystem, Seagrass, Seaweed. He has been extensively involved in field surveys in different ecosystem of the country i.e., Kole wetlands of Kerala, Southern Western Ghats, Eastern Ghats, Great Indian Desert, Andaman & Nicobar Islands. He has published more than 300 research papers in national and international journals and newsletters. He also authored/edited more than 40 books published by respected national and international publishers. He has participated in 36th Indian Scientific Expedition to Antarctica during 2016-2017 and carried out studies on the species abundance and distribution of birds and mammals in Antarctica. He has received Fast Track Young Scientist Fellowship (2006 & 2013) from the Department of Science and Technology, Government of India, New Delhi, Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA) Fellowship (2011), to participate the training on Integrated Biodiversity and Ecosystem Management at Universiti of Malaysia Sabah, Sabah, Malaysia, 36th Indian Scientific Expedition to Antarctica during December 2016 – April 2017 to study the Birds & Mammals of Southern Ocean & Antarctica (Larseman Hill & Schirmachir Oasis). Fellow Award from Andaman Science Association, Andaman & Nicobar Islands for outstanding contribution, Best performance and outstanding contribution award in the Island Tourism Festival by the Andaman & Nicobar Administration. Dr. Sivaperuman has received the highest award of Lt. Governor’s Commendation Certificate of Andaman & Nicobar Islands for recognising his exemplary contribution towards biodiversity conservation 0n 26th January 2023 Best performance in the Indira Gandhi National Forests Academy, Dehra Dun for delivering lecture for the IFS probationer & in service officers, Fellow Award from the Indian Society of Orchidology for outstanding contribution in the Islands ecosystem. He has delivered a talk on “Human-Crocodile conflict in Neyyar Wildlife Sanctuary” in Asianet Global TV (in Malayalam); Jaya TV (in Tamil); Delivered a talk on “Faunal diversity of Andaman and Nicobar Islands” in Doordharsan, Port Blair (in Tamil); Delivered an interview in Rajya Sabha (RSTV) on 27th July 2019 about the activities & achievement of ANRC/ZSI; “Human-Crocodile conflict in Neyyar Wildlife Sanctuary” National Geographic TV (Earth pulse series II – Episode 4). He has reported more than 80 new records to India, Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Kerala & Great Indian Desert from various faunal group especially birds, mammals, moths, wasp, odonates. He also reported new species of frog, tree shrew, termites, earthworm, butterflies, moth etc. He has inspected more than 50 research labs of animal house facilities such as pharmaceutical companies, Medical College, Veterinary College, arts & science college. He was main force to bring the guidelines for the animal house facilities and syllabus for the training course in respect of experimentation on animals. He is a recognized supervisor for Ph.D. research under the Pondicherry University and nine candidate completed Ph.D., and four are under pursuing. Also, he has guided for several Master degree, and Under graduate students for dissertation and internships. He has successfully conducted certificate course on Para-Taxonomist including PBR; Bird Identification and Basic Ornithology under the Green Skill Development Programme of Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, Government of India, training workshop on Costal and Marine Biodiversity of Island ecosystem sponsored by MoEF&CC. Workshop on Bird identification and Basic ornithology under accelerate vigyan by SERB, Department of Science and Technology. Training for IFS Probationers, and in-service IFS officers since 2017 for the Indira Gandhi National Forest Academy on Biodiversity of A & N Islands and possible collaboration with ZSI. Training on schedule animals to the CISF posted in Airport & Sea Port in Andaman (Two batches) during 2018; Indian Coast Guard, IRBn, Andaman and Nicobar Police, Andaman and Nicobar Forests Department. He has organised many National and International seminar/symposium/workshop in Andaman and Nicobar Islands, and also coordinated in mainland. He is a State Coordinator for Asian Waterbirds Census, conducted by Wetland International and Bombay Natural History Society. He has participated and presented research papers at 100 national and international seminars/ symposia/webinars. He is life member of various scientific societies in India and abroad. He also successfully completed 10 major research projects funded by SERB, Department of Science and Technology (Fast Tract Young Scientist Scheme), Ministry of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change, Ministry of Defence, besides several internal research programmes. Conceptualized and bringing “National Coral Reef Research Institute” to the islands, through Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change, Government of India which would give recognition to the islands not only Nationally but also Globally. In addition, it is also proposed to develop state-of-the-art Interpretation Centre to showcase marine biodiversity, Live Aquarium, Coral Museum. He has Established Molecular Systematics Laboratory in ZSI, Port Blair which will increase our understanding of eco-evolutionary processes of the long-isolated, environmentally diverse, and rich biodiversity of this island ecosystem. Initiated to creation of ENVIS Centre (Environmental Information System) in ZSI, Port Blair through MoEF&CC, Government of India. Through ENVIS centre the following works will be disseminated, i.e., inventory of authentic information on the Biodiversity of A&N Islands, providing baseline information for the researchers, academicians etc., undertake various application-oriented research on biodiversity, awareness workshop, seminar to the school, college students and general public, ENVIS Centre will also be helpful to identify an area to promote eco-tourism and to enhance the revenue of government. Recently, Hon’ble Union Home Ministry has inaugurated the National Repository for Island Faunal Diversity, which host the voucher specimens of fauna of Andaman and Nicobar Islands, and adjacent southeast Asian countries. These voucher specimens form the basis for research on evolution, speciation, distribution, taxonomy, systematics, ecology and evolutionary biology. Besides, this repository building also will be used as museum through LED display of endemic, endangered fauna of this archipelago.

ap.about: Dr. Akhilesh Kumar Singh possesses over a decade of teaching experience and currently holds the position of Professor within the Department of Mechanical Engineering at Aditya College of Engineering (A) in Surampalem, Andhra Pradesh, India. His academic journey culminated in a Ph.D. in Production Engineering, which he earned from the esteemed National Institute of Technology (NIT) Agartala, Tripura, India. Dr. Singh has made significant contributions to the field of mechanical engineering, exemplified by his numerous publications in esteemed national and international journals, as well as presentations at conferences. Additionally, his innovative mindset has resulted in the acquisition of eight patents. His research interests span a wide spectrum, encompassing research collaboration with industries, consultancy, advancements in Green Energy, design and fabrication of cutting-edge equipment, tools, and techniques for research purposes, and progress in welding and manufacturing techniques. In recognition of his expertise, Dr. Singh serves as an esteemed editorial board member and a diligent reviewer for several academic journals. Furthermore, he actively engages as a speaker at both national and international conferences. Dr. Akhilesh Kumar Singh is a distinguished member of prestigious professional organizations, including IWS (Indian Welding Society), IEI (Institution of Engineers India), and IAENG (International Association of Engineers). His multifaceted career underscores his commitment to advancing knowledge and fostering innovation in the realm of mechanical engineering.

ar.about: Miss Neelam Rupa presently working as an external faculty Sociology Department, Arunachal Pradesh State Nursing College, TRIHMS Arunachal Pradesh. She has wide experience in teaching and social work for last 10 years. She served as an Asst.Professor in the esteemed educational institutions of the state like Jawahar Lal Nehru College(JNC) Pasighat, Don Bosco College(DBC) Itanagar, St. Francis De Sales College (SFSC) Aalo, Arunachal Pradesh. She has also worked with various local and national NGOs for the welfare of women, Children and Environment. She is an active member of Arunachal Pradesh Women Welfar Society (APWWS) and MAGIC Club( Making Arunachal Green Incredible and Clean)- state renowned NGO. She did her MPhil and Masters degree in Sociology from Rajiv Gandhi University a lone central university in Arunachal Pradesh. She is a Gold Medalist in her Bachelor’s and Masters degree. She is also a recipient of State Governor’s Award for academic excellence

as.about: I completed graduation (Economics) LLB and MA(Rural Development). I am also founder General Secretary of Social Development Forum SDF (NGO) in 2012 working in 11district of Assam.

br.about: I, Dr. Rupak Kumar, a member expert of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization inclusive policy lab earned a doctoral degree from BITS Pilani, India in 2018 and acquired a visiting fellowship at University of Haifa, Israel under Ministry of Education, Govt. of India in 2017. I am a researcher, innovator currently focused on researching directed evolution of enzymes and developing point of detection device for the estimation of procarcionogen cum glazing agent on coated fruits and vegetables. Also, biological remediation methods for organic and inorganic pollutants from complex environmental matrices, mainly polluted water and sediments are also core interest. My research, teaching duties and professional experience in the academic and industry sector, are related to environmental analytical methods development, pollutant removal and applied microbiology. My article namely toxic gloss on fresh veggie plate got print media attention and was highly appreciated in national and international platform and quite popular among common audience. I have been associated with various project work at India premier institute like Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR), Indian Institute of Technology-Delhi (IITD), Advanced Centre for Treatment, Research and Education in Cancer, Tata Memorial Center, Mumbai (ACTREC-TMC, Mumbai), National Dope Testing Laboratory (NDTL) under Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, Govt. of India and BITS Pilani, Hyderabad Campus, Hyderabad (BPHC), MIGAL Galilee Research Institute, Israel at different capacities during my academics.

ct.about: I hold a diverse academic background with a B.Sc in Biology, an M.Sc and M.Phil in Biotechnology with a thesis focused on “Micropropagation and Flavonoid Production of Momordica Charantia,” followed by a Ph.D. in Biotechnology, where my research centered on “Removal of Heavy Metals, Dye Degradation, and Water Purification using Water Hyacinth.” I have a wealth of teaching experience in microbiology and biotechnology and have actively contributed to research projects such as rice biofortification under a DBT sponsorship. Additionally, I have worked on the development of low-cost, highly nutritious whey-based beverages to combat malnutrition among women and girls in rural areas through an RKVY-ICAR sponsored project. My commitment to community betterment is reflected in my involvement in various NGOs and social work initiatives.

dl.about: Satya Pal, an accomplished professional, serves as an Assistant Health Education Officer with a commitment to enhancing community health. Having pursued a Tuberculosis Health Visitor course from the Tuberculosis Association of India, Satya Pal possesses specialized expertise in addressing critical health concerns. Alongside this, the completion of a Diploma in Pharmacy from CRRIT Kanjhawla Delhi adds a strong foundation in pharmaceutical knowledge. With a holistic approach to community welfare, Satya Pal holds a Master’s in Social Work (MSW) from TGOU and a Certificate in NGO Management from IGNOU, showcasing a dedication to social upliftment. Furthermore, the pursuit of a Postgraduate Diploma in Environment and Occupational Health (PGDEOH) reflects a concern for well-being beyond traditional health domains. The acquisition of a Certificate in Rural Journalism from UPRTOU further exemplifies Satya Pal’s multifaceted skill set, allowing for effective communication and advocacy on pertinent health and social issues.

hr.about: I’m Jahul Khan from Haryana MEWAT a professional with a B.Com and an MBA in Travel and Tourism Management (TTM), boasts a wealth of experience in the tourism industry. My expertise lies in the tourism operations department, where i has contributed significantly to the success of Rayna Tours through my dedication and knowledge.

hp.about: Dr. B.S. Negi is working as Faculty Member, Rural Development in the Department of Interdisciplinary Studies at Himachal Pradesh University, Shimla, INDIA. He did his post-Graduation (2003), M. Phil (2005) & Ph. D in Public Administration (2008) from the H.P. University, Shimla. He has completed Post Graduate Diploma in Geo-Spatial Technology Application in Rural Development (PGGARD), NIRDPR, 2020-21 through correspondence. He is pursuing master course in Corporate Social Responsibility (MACSR) from IGNOU. He joined the Institute of Integrated Himalayan Studies (UGC Centre of Excellence) in 2005 as Project Fellow, later as Project Officer in 2009 and after the upgradation of same institute as Department of Interdisciplinary and started teaching assignments to teach the MBA-Rural Development (in 2013), supervising M. Phil – Rural Development (2018-19) and Ph. D (2019). Apart from his core responsibility at H.P. University as Faculty Member, Dr. Negi working as an associate editor of Himachal Pradesh University Journal of Social Sciences and Humanities, coordinator IIRS-ORP Network Institute. He was awarded H.P. University Junior Research Fellowship in 2004. Indian Institute of Advanced Study (IIAS) awarded associateship in March 2022 (First Spell) & March 2023 (Second Spell). Indian Institute of Remote Sensing, Dehradun awarded appreciated in 2018 & 2020 for discharging the responsibility of coordinator IIRS-Outreach Programmes Network Institute. His area of research and interest are Good Governance, Rural Development and Local Self Government. He has supervised around 30 PG dissertations, 2 M. Phil and guiding 2 Ph.D. candidates. He has published 26 research papers in peer reviewed and UGC care listed Journals and published 5 books and some news articles. Dr. Negi has attended more than 100 training programme / summer school / winter school / autumn school / research methodology course / orientation programme etc. offline and online from reputed institutes like ICSSR, DAAD, GIZ, HBF, IIPA, NIRDPR, IGNOU, NCRI, UNITAR-CIFAL, IPSA, AARDO, IIRS, ISI, Lokniti, Doon University, NITTTR, HIPA & SIRD etc. He is member of many professional bodies such as Indian Adult Education Association (IAEA), Indian Association of Social Science & Health (IASSH), Indian Political Science Association (IPSA), Indian Society of Remote Sensing (ISRS), Association of Indian Political Economic Association (AIPEA), Association of Professional Social Work and Development Practitioners (APSWDP), People for Responsible Governance (PeRGo), Himachal Gyan Vigyan Samiti (HGVS) and GFSRD.

jk.about: A Doctorate in Ag. Extension, presently working as an Assistant professor/Jr. Scientist/ Subject Matter Specialist in Ag. Extension at SKUAST-K, also has a Post graduate Diploma in Rural Development. With about 10 years of experience in research, teaching and extension has worked extensively on promotion of sustainable agriculture. As a prolific writer, have been contributing through various newspapers by writing on issues related to rural development, climate change, SDGs, farm policies, agri. programmes, entrepreneurship in agriculture, women issues in agriculture, sustainable agriculture, farmers mobilization and a host of other issues.

ka.about: Prof. Nagraju L G. Assistant Professor, School of Commerce and Management studies , REVA University, holds an M.Com degree from Bangalore University with Accounting and Taxation as subjects of specialization, Master of Tourism Management (MTM) from IGNOU, and has qualified UGC NET and JRF in Commerce and also qualified Karnataka state Eligibility Test (K-SET) in Management Stream. Presently he is pursuing Ph.D from REVA University in Social Entrepreneurship Domain. He worked as Assistant Professor in Vivekananda Degree Evening College Bangalore for the period of three years and in St. Claret College Bangalore for one year in the Department of Tourism as Co-coordinator. He also worked as visiting faculty in various institutions. He attended and published various Research Papers like in Indexed journals and presented papers in State, National and International conferences and also participated Faculty Development programmes in IITs and IIMs. He also worked for NSDC program from for two years in the domain of Retail Management and Tally ERP9, he attended more than 20 Faculty development program from various Institutions in the domain of Tourism development in the state, digitalisation of agriculture, Farmers empowerment, small Business development , women’s empowerment etc. He presented more than 25 research papers in many universities and institutions in India. He also worked as a research assistant for Kingston University United Kingdom-REVA Joint Research Project Indian MSMEs’ Responses to the Covid-19 Pandemic Crisis. He completed more than 20 online webinar from Indian and global universities

kl.about: Rajesh. E is Assistant Professor in Special Education, School of Behavioural Sciences & Joint Director, Inter University Centre for Disability Studies at Mahatma Gandhi University, Kottayam, Kerala, India. He has 16 years of teaching experience. His qualifications include M.Sc, M.A.Education, B.Ed Special Education and Masters Diploma in Counselling Psychology. He is a Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI) certified Professional. At present he is member in Board of Studies of School of Behavioural Sciences, Mahatma Gandhi University and formerly member of P.G Board of Studies, Pedagogical Sciences, Mahatma Gandhi University as well as Board of Studies, Psychology, Kannur University.Hecurrently serves as Editorial Board Member in Nepal Journal of Epidemiology, Frontiers in Epidemiology, Journal for ReAttach Therapy and Developmental Diversities. He is also reviewer of International Journal of Basic and Applied Sciences and Journal of Clinical & Diagnostic Research.

mp.about: Dr. Kamakshi Maheshwari, working as Asst. Prof in Indian Institute of Tourism and Travel Management, Gwalior. She had PhD on the research topic “Development and Management of Heritage Tourism in Gwalior Region”, Jiwaji University. She is the Nodal officer of Central Nodal Agency – Rural Tourism and Rural Homestay and Central Nodal Agency – Adventure Tourism under Ministry of Tourism Government of India. She is the Ex- Governing Council Member of INTACH since 2009 and the president of WICCI Rural Tourism Council (M.P.). She is coordinating in many prestigious projects of Government of India like Swachhta Action Plan, Ek Bharat Shreshta Bharat, Incredible India Tourist Facilitator and many more. She has been awarded as Outstanding Young Person of India by JCI in 2021. She is a LIMCA book award holder in 2008, 2010 and 2012.

mh.about: 1. Ph.D in commerce from S.N.D.T. Women’s University, Mumbai. 2. Completed 3 minor research projects under University of Mumbai in 2011-12, 2012-2013 and 2015-2016. 3. Appointed as judge for 12th & 17th Inter-Collegiate/ Institute/ Department Avishkar Research Convention: 2012-23 & 2017-18 for Zonal Round by University of Mumbai. 4. 1. A Novel Long Rage IOT Based Automatic Dispenser System for Heavy Water Line Flow Control Patent No: 2021101961. Authority issuing patent: Commonwealth of Australia, Patent office, Australian government & 2. IOT Based Solar Assisted Climatic Reader Farming Robot Patent No: 202131026443 Authority issuing patent: Official Journal of Patent Office, Government of India. 5. Recipient of Vocational Excellence Award 2020 in Education by Rotary Club of Mumbai West Coast. (For taking up community development projects) 6. Individual SPSC Ambassador (Sustainable Procurement & Supply Chain Ambassador, U.K.). 7. Ph.D. Guide in Accountancy under University of Mumbai (Center K.P.B.Hinduja College of Commerce)

nl.about: Dr. Razouleu Caroline Sorunuo National Coordinator, National Media Coordinator SIETIIE Innovation & Research Organization www.sietiie.com Former IB Principal and CBSE K12 Principal, Professional Phonetician, Soft Skill Trainer and Guest Lecturer to Nagaland Tool-Room & Training Centre http://www.nttcdimapur.org.in/, Language Teacher, Proof Reader, Professional Phonetician, served as NGO Administrator for more than a decade to SSMS India www.ssmsindia.org. and KUDATech Skill Centre, Dimapur: Nagaland, worked as a Reporter, and an Investigator for Humanitarian Act Press accredited by Times of India, served as the Chief Bureau to Future Alert Magazine (FA) North-East: India. Served and serving multiple organizations on Regional, State, National, and International levels as Administrator, and Active member for more than a decade. Regular Volunteer of GoCamp Ukraine https://www.gocamps.in/, Member of Global Teachers Club https://global.oup.com/, Member of INDLET (International Dialogue on English Language Teaching), Member of TEFL (Teaching English as a second or Foreign Language) Kuwait www.teflkuwait.com, a Brigadier to GDP Global Play Brigade https://www.globalplaybrigade.org/contact-us, Member of KSAALT TESOL (Kingdom of Saudi Arabia Association of Language Teachers) and many more.

or.about: Dr. Patita Paban Mohanty is an academic and researcher with 19 years of enriched experience in university education, research, training, and consultancy. He holds a doctorate degree from Siksha O Anusandhan Deemed to be University, Odisha, India. An Indian professor, with a distinctive array of research papers in national and international peer-reviewed journals indexed in Scopus and other eminent databases.

pb.about: Dr (Ms) Sonia Sharma educational qualifications as B.Sc., B.Ed., M.Sc. M.Ed., MA Sociology, UGC NET, Ph.D., regular under ICSSR Scholarship, two years computer diploma in “Teacher Training”. Having more than 17 years’ experience in the field of academics and administration. She started her career as a school teacher and further lead to Coordinator, Vice Principal and Principal, Academic Counsellor of IGNOU, and worked as Assistant Professor in many reputed colleges/institutes/universities During Journey achieved and nominated many awards. Worked on many SDGs under community development programs. Having feministic approach in research, more than 85 multidisciplinary publications, attended more than 530 online/ offline conferences/seminars including international and national with paper presentations and publications as well as a participant. Invited as guest lecture/keynote speaker/resource persons in many reputed journals, verified reviewer of many reputed journals, life time membership of many educational bodies, Awarded, Ministry of Education, Ministry of Information Broadcasting, Ministry of MSME, “Exceptional contribution as a Primary Evaluator in TOYCATHON 2021”, Inspiring Award (As An active Principal) International by Olympiad Of English Language2012, to enhance academic level of students in English language by IOEL,2012 , Global faculty award for extraordinary teaching acumen, unstoppable educator ‘s spirit, remarkable professional skills and contribution in the field of education, 2022, AKS Education Awards., Chanakya Award 2023 on 25th March 2023, on one day International multidisciplinary conference on “Emerging trends and challenges, Gandhi memorial national college, Ambala Cant. in auditorium, jointly organized by International Council for Education, Research and Training (ICERT), act as supervisor of Ph.D. and master students on various current topics,

rj.about: My self Dr. Anita Jain, Head, Department of Botany, Vidya Bhawan Rural Institute, Udaipur is an active researcher pursuing research in the field of Ethnobotany, Plant Taxonomy and Assessment of Biodiversity since last 25 years. She completed her Ph.D. in 2000 under the supervision of Dr. S.S. Katewa, Ex.Prof. & Head, Dept. of Botany, MLSU, Udaipur. She is engaged in Assessment and Conservation of Biodiversity, inventorization, documentation and validation of folk wisdom of tribals and rural communities of Rajasthan. She prepared encyclopaedia on Ethnomedicinal and Ethnoveterinary knowledge of tribals of Rajasthan. She also prepared ‘Bibliography of Ethnobotany of India’ in 2016. She is NABET approved ‘A’ grade Functional Area Expert in EB and Soil conservation. Recently she visited Denmark as a Part of Indian delegation under the Green strategic Partnership between the govt. of India and Govt. of Denmark as Biodiversity expert for the project Rejuvenation of Gumaniya Wala-Nala” and development of green space in urban area. She give consultancy to Forest Department, IWRM, Udaipur, and several private companies such as Hindustan Zinc, JK cement, Birla cement, Rajasthan to prepare conservation plan of biodiversity and development program for the upliftmen/livelihood options of tribal and rural people of Rajasthan. She guided students to complete their doctorate degree. She delivered her research work in front of science fraternity in several international and national conferences, symposia, and seminars. She is invited as resources person in various training programs. Dr. Jain has published 4 books & chapters both in reference as well as text books and published more than 50 research papers in various national and international Journals in her credit. Dr. Jain is recipient of several awards & medals -Young Scientist award in 2002, Dr. Vishwnathan Mudgal medal 2010, Dr. D.C. Pal medal 2012 and NESA “Scientist of the year award -2020”. She successfully completed several research projects funded by MoEF, CSIR, DST and Institute of Ethnobiology, Gwalior, Municipality of Aahrus, Denmark. From beginning Dr. Jain engaged in several academic and extension activities delivered expert and extension lectures on various fora to create awareness for the promotion and conservation of TK, traditional practices and biodiversity to all the category of stake holders including students, farmers, medicine men, tribals and rural people, academician, scientist etc.

tn.about: As a Teacher of Economics, I completed my graduate, post graduate and Ph.D. in Economics from prestigious University of Madras, Chennai, Tamil Nadu, and India. Since my early MA and Ph.D., I have been nurturing a dream to become a scholar and teacher of Economics. My vicinity where I grew up is full of diverse Economical elements and which fascinated me a lot to ponder over various aspects of Economics field from my college and University days. For achieving that goal, I started my teaching journey in the Department of Economics and Rural Development, Alagappa University, Karaikudi, Tamil Nadu, where I have acquired all primary and fundamental knowledge of teaching in Economics and Rural Development and my academic journey has been containing from here in 2016 to present. Morethen 10 years of teaching and research experience in the field of economics, labour, demographic and rural development. i have completed projects from ICSSR, published books and edited chapters related to my field of expertise, i have publishied articles in national and international level, partiicipated and presented papers in natinal and internationl level.

tg.about: MSW,NET/JRF/SET. MPA,SET Senior Research Fellow (SRF), Publons Academy Graduate & Certified Peer Reviewer (Web of Science), Elsevier Certified Peer Reviewer & Assistant Professor and Research Methods from University of London (SOAS). Doctorand in Department of Social Work, Osmania University, Hyderabad, India. Under the award of UGC-NET/JRF Fellowship, sponsored by University Grants Commission (UGC). Research interest in Social Sciences; Social Work Research; Sustainable Agriculture;. Environmental Studies; Gender studies; Public policy and Ethnographic Studies. 2 years of teaching experience as Assistant professor, ratified by Osmania University Selection Committee (OUSC)

tr.about: Dr. Annesha Saha is working as an Assistant Professor in Faculty of Management & Commerce, The ICFAI University, Tripura since 2011. She is also leading Commerce Department in the same University as HOD, B.Com since 2019. Along with that she has been teaching different subjects of commerce and management for more than last twelve years. She did her B.Com (H) in the year 2005 from Assam University (A Central University), M.Com in the year 2007 and awarded Ph.D Degree in December 2016 from Tripura University (A Central University). Her area of interest is Accounting, Finance, Business Management and Rural Entrepreneurship. She has published many research articles in the journals of national and international repute including ABDC and Scopus indexed. She has also authored of three books till date. She is acting as an editorial board member of Journal of Advance Research and Development and IIP Edited Book Series and experience of organising seminars, conferences and organisational events as a convenor at university levels.

up.about: Dr Bhavna Wal has had her early schooling from Loreto Convent, Lucknow and higher education from Lucknow University. She is a double PG in Mass Comm and Human Rights. She has pursued her PhD in Humanities and Natural Sciences specializing in Child Labor with a special focus on Nobel Laureate Mr Kailash Satyarti’s NGO Bachpan Bachao Andolan as a role model. The author has worked in various editorial and administrative capacities during her tenure as a journalist. A story teller at heart, she has written on a plethora of subjects covering a wide range of topics – – hard news, features and socio-cultural subjects. Now an aspirational educator teaching mass communication to undergraduates and postgraduates, she uses her rich field experience to teach journalism to create that classroom impact on prospective content creators.

ut.about: Dedicated researcher in Environmental Geography looking for an opportunity to get a responsible position in academics to encourage creativity and increase performance. Combines a focus on student achievement with a passion for scholarly work presenting and publishing at conferences both national and international. I got additional certifications in GIS and Remote Sensing software to specialize in mapping. I am a Master of Art (M.A.) in Geography, Master of Science (M.Sc.) in Remote Sensing and GIS, and Doctorate (Ph.D.) in Spatio-Temporal Change of Snow and Vegetation Cover in Gori Ganga Watershed, Kumaun Himalaya by Using Remote Sensing and GIS Techniques which The keywords of my thesis: Spatio-Temporal Change, Snow Cover/Line, Vegetation Cover/Line, TimberCover/Line, Gori Ganga Watershed, Remote Sensing and GIS.

wb.about: I am a teacher of Veterinary Extension Education with 17 years of Research, Extension and teaching experience in ICAR institute and University state.
